is_p_falciparum.evidence: This is a study of human interferon gamma (IFN-γ) gene polymorphisms in relation to schistosomiasis, not P. falciparum. No Plasmodium parasite genetics involved.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports human IFN-γ SNPs at positions +2109, +3810, +5134 — not P. falciparum drug-resistance markers.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Allelic and genotype frequencies given (e.g. 95/134 A/A) but for human IFN-γ, not parasite markers.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from a Sudanese village (Meisseria and Rawashda tribes), but data are human host genetics, irrelevant to P. falciparum markers.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No relevant collection timing for parasite markers; study is human genetics.

african_field_samples.evidence: Sudanese population samples collected in an African country; but samples are human DNA, not parasite isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study describing new SNPs.

duplicate_risk.evidence: No indication of parasite dataset overlap; not applicable to this human genetics study.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary parasite data referenced; paper is entirely about human IFN-γ SNPs.

assessment.summary: This paper describes three novel human interferon gamma (IFN-γ) gene polymorphisms in a Sudanese population studied for schistosomiasis susceptibility. It contains no P. falciparum drug-resistance marker data whatsoever.
exclusion_reasons: Not a P. falciparum study — reports human IFN-γ gene polymorphisms
No curated P. falciparum drug-resistance markers reported
Data pertain to human host genetics, not parasite field isolates
